hangingPieces.label: Hanging pieces
hangingPieces.summary: Moves that left a piece where it could simply be captured.
hangingPieces.advice: Before you commit to a move, do one last scan: "what can my opponent take?" That single habit removes most of these losses.

missedMates.label: Missed checkmates
missedMates.summary: Positions where a forced mate was available but a slower move was played.
missedMates.advice: When the enemy king looks airy, pause and hunt checks first — forcing moves are free to calculate because the replies are limited.

missedForks.label: Missed forks
missedForks.summary: Chances to attack two pieces at once that went unplayed.
missedForks.advice: Knights and pawns are the usual culprits — each time a knight can jump forward, check whether the landing square eyes two targets.

missedTactics.label: Missed tactics
missedTactics.summary: Winning shots — captures, pins, skewers, discovered attacks — that slipped by.
missedTactics.advice: Loose pieces and lined-up pieces are tactical fuel. Scan for undefended targets and pieces sharing a line before settling for a quiet move.

openingMistakes.label: Opening slips
openingMistakes.summary: Eval given away in the first phase, right out of (or just after) theory.
openingMistakes.advice: You don't need more memorised lines — develop toward the centre, castle early, and don't move the same piece twice without a reason.

endgameMistakes.label: Endgame conversion
endgameMistakes.summary: Advantages that shrank or slipped away once the board emptied.
endgameMistakes.advice: In simple positions, king activity and passed pawns decide games. Slow down — endgames reward one careful check more than fast instinct.

weekly.quiet: A quiet week on the board so far — nothing logged yet. One puzzle or a quick lesson today is all it takes to get the week moving.

weekly.streak_other: Your streak is at {{count}} days — keep it alive.
